name: Pork Pinwheels with Apricot Stuffing
recipeInstructions:
1 Split the pork tenderloin lengthwise, cutting to but not through the opposite side; open it out flat.
2 Pound the tenderloin lightly with a meat mallet to form a 10x6 rectangle.
3 For the apricot stuffing: Dissolve chicken bouillon granules in hot water, pour over apricots. Let stand for 5 minutes.
4 Cook celery and chopped onions in margarine until tender but not brown. Remove from heat; stir in cinnamon and black pepper.
5 In a large bowl, mix bread cubes, onion mixture, and apricot mixture; toss lightly to moisten.
6 Spread the stuffing evenly over the pork tenderloin.
7 Roll up the pork into a jelly-roll style roll, starting from the short side. Secure with wooden toothpicks or tie with string at 1-inch intervals.
8 Cut the meat roll into six 1-inch slices. Place the slices on a rack in an unheated broiler pan, cut side down.
9 Broil 4 inches from heat for 12 minutes, then turn and broil for 11 to 13 more minutes or until done.
10 Remove toothpicks or string; transfer meat slices to a serving platter.
11 For the sauce: Combine cornstarch and nutmeg. Stir in apricot nectar. Cook and stir until bubbly. Cook and stir for 2 more minutes.
12 Serve the sauce with the meat slices.
